worth_it_explanation: Amazon Marketing Cloud is where cross-media, privacy-safe measurement happens on Amazon DSP, and the standard reports cannot answer the path-to-purchase and overlap questions it can. An instance that is provisioned but never queried, or one that ingests only part of the advertiser's data, leaves those questions unanswered while the cost of the instance is still paid. These checks read the instance configuration and query activity in the console, which is fast.
intro: Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) is a clean room that holds pseudonymized, event-level signals from Amazon DSP and other Amazon Ads products, so an advertiser can run custom analyses across media without handling user-level data directly. It answers questions the prebuilt reports do not, such as how Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads work together on the path to purchase. These checks confirm that the instance exists where the advertiser needs it, that it ingests the right data sources, and that its outputs are used rather than left as one-off analyses.

audit_checks.title: Amazon Marketing Cloud is not set up where cross-media measurement is needed
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without a clean room, the advertiser measures Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads in separate report views and cannot see overlap, sequencing, or combined path to purchase. Budget decisions are made on a partial picture, and the incremental value of each tactic stays unknown.

educate.base:
Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) is a clean room that holds pseudonymized, event-level records of impressions, clicks, and conversions from Amazon DSP and other Amazon Ads products. The advertiser writes queries against that data without seeing any individual user, which makes it the place to answer cross-media questions that the prebuilt reports cannot, for example how a Sponsored Products click and an Amazon DSP view-through combine before a purchase.

An advertiser running Amazon DSP at scale alongside Sponsored Ads, or one that wants custom attribution and audience analysis, gains measurement from AMC that the standard reports do not provide. A campaign measured only through the default reports sees each product in isolation, so overlap and sequencing stay invisible and the combined effect cannot be read.

A few signs point to AMC being absent where it would help:
  • The advertiser runs both Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads but reports on them separately with no combined view
  • Questions about reach overlap, path to purchase, or incrementality come up and go unanswered
  • No AMC instance is provisioned, or one exists but shows no recent query activity
You may want to confirm whether the measurement questions the advertiser is asking can be answered by the standard reports before concluding an instance is needed. AMC earns its place when the questions are cross-media.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a seller or vendor advertiser, Amazon Marketing Cloud is where the relationship between upper-funnel Amazon DSP and lower-funnel Sponsored Products can be measured against actual purchases. You may want to confirm an instance is provisioned and that recent queries connect the two, rather than reporting each product alone.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser driving off-Amazon outcomes, an Amazon Marketing Cloud instance can join Amazon DSP exposure to the Amazon Ad Tag conversion signal. You may want to confirm the off-Amazon conversion feed reaches the instance so the analysis is not limited to on-Amazon events.

learn_more.source_title: A complete guide to Amazon Marketing Cloud

investigate.intro: Confirm whether an Amazon Marketing Cloud instance exists for the advertiser and whether it is queried, then weigh that against the measurement questions the advertiser needs answered.

amazon_dsp_console.label: Amazon DSP console
steps: Check whether the advertiser has an Amazon Marketing Cloud instance provisioned and linked to the Amazon DSP advertiser.
If an instance exists, review recent query activity to see whether it is used or sitting idle.
List the measurement questions the advertiser is asking and note which ones the standard reports cannot answer.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether an Amazon Marketing Cloud instance exists, whether it shows recent query activity, and whether the advertiser has cross-media questions the standard reports cannot answer. An advertiser with cross-media needs and an active instance passes. An advertiser with cross-media needs and no instance, or an idle one, is a fail recorded with the unanswered questions.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether an instance exists, whether it is queried, and the open measurement questions above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}} runs Amazon DSP without using Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) for cross-media measurement. The standard reports show each Amazon Ads product in isolation, so the overlap and sequencing between Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads on the path to purchase cannot be read, and budget decisions rest on a partial picture. {{voice_recommend}} provisioning an AMC instance, connecting the relevant data sources, and building the queries that answer the cross-media questions the account needs. After setup, confirm the instance returns results that join the products rather than reporting each alone.

audit_checks.title: The Amazon Marketing Cloud instance does not ingest the data sources the analysis needs
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A clean room can only answer what it can see. When an instance is missing the Sponsored Ads feed or the advertiser first-party signals, queries return a partial result that reads as complete, so the analysis understates reach overlap and the contribution of the missing source.

educate.base:
An Amazon Marketing Cloud instance reports on the data sources connected to it. By default it carries the Amazon DSP signals, and additional sources, such as Sponsored Ads and the advertiser's own first-party events uploaded for analysis, are connected separately. A query that spans products returns a complete answer only when every product in the question is feeding the instance.

When a source is missing, the query still runs and returns rows, so the gap is easy to miss. An analysis meant to measure how Amazon DSP and Sponsored Products combine, run against an instance that holds only the Amazon DSP feed, reports the Amazon DSP side alone and reads as if that were the whole story.

A few signs point to a source gap:
  • A cross-product query returns far fewer touchpoints than the campaign volume would suggest
  • The instance configuration lists only the Amazon DSP source when the advertiser also runs Sponsored Ads
  • First-party events the advertiser expected to join against are absent from the schema
You may want to confirm the connected sources against the products the advertiser actually runs before trusting a cross-media result. A missing source does not announce itself in the query output.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a seller or vendor advertiser, the Sponsored Products and Sponsored Brands feeds are usually the sources most likely to be missing since they are connected separately from Amazon DSP. You may want to confirm those feeds are present before reading any Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads overlap analysis.

investigate.intro: Review the instance's connected data sources against the products the advertiser runs, then sanity-check a cross-product query's touchpoint volume.

amazon_marketing_cloud.label: Amazon Marketing Cloud
steps: Open the instance and list the connected data sources.
Compare that list against the Amazon Ads products the advertiser runs, including Sponsored Ads and any uploaded first-party events.
Run or review a cross-product query and check whether the touchpoint counts are consistent with the campaign volume rather than far lower.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the instance's connected sources and the products the advertiser runs. An instance whose sources cover every product in the advertiser's cross-media questions passes. An instance missing a source that a query depends on is a fail recorded with the missing source and the affected analysis.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the connected sources and the products the advertiser runs above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon Marketing Cloud instance does not ingest every data source its cross-media analysis depends on. Queries still return results, so the gap is not obvious, but the output understates the contribution of the missing source and any overlap that involves it. {{voice_recommend}} connecting the missing sources, for example the Sponsored Ads feed or the advertiser first-party events, and re-running the affected queries to confirm the touchpoint volume matches the campaign activity.

audit_checks.title: Amazon Marketing Cloud insights are not activated back into Amazon DSP
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When clean-room findings stay as one-off analyses, the audiences they identify never reach the bidding. The advertiser learns who converts but does not act on it, so the measurement effort produces a slide rather than a targeting change.

educate.base:
Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) supports audience creation, so a query that identifies a valuable segment, such as users who viewed a product several times without purchasing, can be turned into an audience and activated in Amazon DSP. This closes the loop between the analysis and the bidding, rather than leaving the finding in a report.

When the loop is left open, the instance produces insights that no campaign acts on. The advertiser may learn that a particular behavior predicts conversion and never build the audience that would target it, so the analysis informs nobody's bids.

A few signs point to an open loop:
  • AMC queries identify high-value segments that have no matching Amazon DSP audience
  • Audience-creation queries exist in the instance but were never activated to the Amazon DSP advertiser
  • The measurement output is shared as a one-time analysis with no follow-up targeting change
You may want to confirm whether the segments AMC surfaces are being activated rather than read once and set aside. The value of the analysis depends on it reaching the bidding.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a seller or vendor advertiser, an Amazon Marketing Cloud audience of repeat detail-page viewers who have not purchased is a common candidate to activate for a lower-funnel Amazon DSP line item. You may want to confirm such segments are built and activated rather than only described in a report.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a non-endemic advertiser, an Amazon Marketing Cloud audience can capture users who engaged but did not convert off Amazon. You may want to confirm those segments are activated to Amazon DSP rather than left as analysis.

learn_more.source_title: A complete guide to Amazon Marketing Cloud

investigate.intro: Review the audiences AMC has identified and check whether they were activated to the Amazon DSP advertiser.

amazon_marketing_cloud.label: Amazon Marketing Cloud
steps: Review the instance's audience-creation queries and the segments they define.
Check the Amazon DSP advertiser for audiences activated from those queries.
Note any high-value segment that the analysis identified but that has no matching activated audience.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the high-value segments AMC has identified and whether each was activated to Amazon DSP. A segment that is identified and activated passes. A segment identified but never activated is a fail recorded with the segment and the line item that could use it.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the AMC-identified segments and their activation status above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s Amazon Marketing Cloud insights are not activated back into Amazon DSP. The instance identifies segments that predict conversion, but those segments are not built into audiences the bidding can use, so the analysis informs no campaign. {{voice_recommend}} turning the highest-value segments into Amazon Marketing Cloud audiences and activating them to the Amazon DSP advertiser, then confirming the matching line items target them.
